steps.verify: A portion of the waveform is highlighted/selected in the audio track
steps.instruction: Select a few seconds of audio that contains only background noise (no talking or other content)

steps.verify: The Noise Reduction dialog appears with Step 1 completed; cursor moves to Step 2
steps.instruction: Click the 'Get Noise Profile' button in Step 1 of the Noise Reduction dialog

steps.verify: Settings displayed in dialog: 12 in Noise reduction field, 6.00 in Sensitivity, 3 in Frequency smoothing
steps.instruction: In Step 2, adjust the three sliders: Noise reduction (12 dB), Sensitivity (6.00), Frequency smoothing (3 bands)

steps.verify: Audio plays through speakers showing the reduced noise effect
steps.instruction: Click the 'Preview' button to hear how the noise reduction will sound

steps.verify: Dialog closes and the waveform may show visual changes; audio plays with reduced background noise
steps.instruction: Click the 'OK' button to apply the noise reduction to the selected audio

steps.verify: The menu bar in the screenshot reads 'Audacity' immediately before and after any menu-bar click, and the intended Audacity menu/dialog is what actually opened
steps.milestone: Guard against losing focus to another app
steps.instruction: Before clicking Effect, Noise Reduction, or any other menu-bar item, confirm the top menu bar reads 'Audacity', not another app. On this machine, other open apps (e.g. a code editor or chat app) have been observed stealing frontmost focus mid-session, causing a click meant for Audacity's menu to land in that other app instead. The attached screenshot is a real example from a prior failed run: it was captured right after a click intended for Audacity's Effect menu, but the frontmost app had silently changed to a code editor instead. If this happens, do not just Cmd+Tab blindly and re-click the same way - click directly on the Audacity window itself (or select it by name via Cmd+Tab) to restore focus, then retry the exact same menu action.

steps.verify: The same dialog or error message does not appear more than once for the same intended action; if it does, a genuinely different selection/interaction method was tried before retrying, not a repeat of the same one
steps.milestone: Treat a recurring identical screen as proof of being stuck, not a fluke to retry past
steps.instruction: If the exact same dialog or error re-appears after already trying to resolve it once (for example, Audacity's 'No Audio Selected' dialog appearing again even though the selection timer/display still shows a nonzero duration and the track shows a visible highlighted region), that repetition is itself the signal: the selection is not being registered the way it looks on screen, and dismissing the dialog to immediately retry the identical Effect-menu path will just reproduce it a third time. Do not repeat the same click sequence. Instead make the selection again using a genuinely different method - e.g. a single click on the track first to establish focus, then a slower deliberate click-and-hold-drag past the intended end point before releasing, or using Select > Selection / keyboard selection instead of a mouse drag - before reopening the effect dialog.

action: Remove background noise from a podcast recording using Audacity's Noise Reduction effect
distilled_by: watching (local Chrome, real account), not transcript
